mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market and strong consumer confidence, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate continued economic growth.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in periods where UNRATE is below 5.0% for more than 60% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) is significantly elevated, it signals an overheating economy, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which typically leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL is above 230 over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with an accommodative monetary policy (low FEDFUNDS) indicates robust consumer demand and ample liquidity, which directly boosts corporate revenues and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the interaction term between RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS level does not significantly correlate with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (p-value > 0.1) over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic recovery, rapid growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signifies strengthening employment and income, leading to increased consumer spending and corporate profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, during periods identified as post-recession recovery, a PAYEMS growth rate exceeding 0.2% MoM is consistently followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals a contraction in future construction activity and broader economic demand, indicating a potential slowdown that negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where HOUST falls below 1000 over any 7-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates healthy manufacturing output and capacity utilization, reflecting strong supply-side conditions that are generally supportive of corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO growth and S&P 500 5-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p-value > 0.1) over any 4-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a late-cycle economy where the central bank is actively tightening monetary policy to curb inflation, leading to reduced liquidity and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.2% in 3 months AND UNRATE < 5.0%
failure_condition: If, when the trigger conditions are met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of observations over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth (GDPC1) in an environment of contained inflation signals a healthy and sustainable economic expansion, providing a strong foundation for corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If periods of GDPC1 growth exceeding 2.0% YoY are consistently followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances over any 6-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.
